prophecies.a:
天数茫茫不可知，鸾台暂借各生知。

白话文：天命渺茫不可预知，暂借扶鸾之台让世人有所知晓。
prophecies.a_en:
The ways of heaven are vast and unknowable; through this spirit writing altar, each life may briefly know.

Vernacular: The workings of fate are boundless and beyond knowing; we temporarily borrow this spirit writing altar so that all may gain some insight.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 此为乩文开篇引言，说明预言以扶乩形式降示，不涉及具体历史事件预测。
prophecies.verdict_en: This is the opening preamble of the divination text, stating that the prophecy is delivered through spirit writing. It does not predict specific historical events.

prophecies.a:
世界干戈终爆发，鼠尾牛头发现时。此次战祸非小可，鸢飞鱼跃也愁眉。

白话文：世界大战终将爆发，在鼠年末牛年初之际显现。这次战祸非同小可，连天上飞的水中游的也为之忧愁。
prophecies.a_en:
World war shall finally erupt, appearing at the tail of the Rat and head of the Ox. This wartime calamity is no small matter — even kites in the sky and fish in the water furrow their brows.

Vernacular: A world war will ultimately break out, manifesting at the turn from the Year of the Rat to the Year of the Ox. This catastrophe of war is extraordinarily grave — even birds in flight and fish leaping from water are stricken with sorrow.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 「鼠尾牛头」指1936年（鼠年）末至1937年（牛年）初。1937年7月7日卢沟桥事变爆发，全面抗日战争开始。二战欧洲战场于1939年爆发。乩文1933年刊出，对战争爆发时间的预言与史实基本吻合。
prophecies.verdict_en: 'Tail of the Rat, head of the Ox' points to late 1936 (Year of the Rat) to early 1937 (Year of the Ox). The Marco Polo Bridge Incident on July 7, 1937 marked the start of the Second Sino-Japanese War. WWII in Europe began in 1939. Published in 1933, the prophecy's timing aligns closely with historical events.

prophecies.a:
天下生灵西复东，可怜遍地是哀鸿。尸填沟壑无人拾，血染山河满地红。天下重武不重文，那怪环球乱纷纷。

白话文：天下百姓从西到东流离失所，可怜到处都是悲苦的难民。尸体填满沟壑无人收拾，鲜血染红了山河大地。天下崇尚武力不崇尚文治，难怪全球动荡不安。
prophecies.a_en:
Living souls drift from west to east under heaven; how pitiable — wailing refugees cover every land. Corpses fill ravines with none to gather them; blood stains mountains and rivers, the earth runs red. The world prizes martial force over civil virtue — no wonder the whole globe is in turmoil.

Vernacular: The people under heaven are displaced from west to east; pitifully, grief-stricken refugees are everywhere. Bodies fill the ditches with no one to collect them; blood dyes the mountains and rivers, the ground entirely red. The world values military power over scholarship — no wonder the globe is engulfed in chaos.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 准确描述了二战及抗日战争期间的惨况。1937至1945年间，中国军民伤亡超过3500万人，大量百姓从东部沿海向西南后方迁移（「西复东」）。「血染山河满地红」真实反映了南京大屠杀等惨烈战事。
prophecies.verdict_en: Accurately describes the devastation of WWII and the Second Sino-Japanese War. Between 1937 and 1945, Chinese military and civilian casualties exceeded 35 million, with massive population displacement from eastern coastal areas to the southwestern interior. 'Blood stains mountains and rivers' reflects the reality of atrocities including the Nanjing Massacre.

prophecies.a:
人我太阳争北土，美人东渡海波生。十四一心人发奋，水去西方启战争。

白话文：日本人与我争夺北方领土，美国人东渡太平洋掀起波澜。「十四一心」合为「德」字，德国人发奋图强；「水去西方」暗指「法」字，法国引发西方战争。
prophecies.q_en: Japanese Invasion and Great Power Conflicts
prophecies.a_en:
The people of the sun contend with us for northern lands; the Americans cross eastward, raising waves upon the sea. 'Fourteen-one-heart' — that people strives with vigor; 'water goes west' — from there war is launched.

Vernacular: The Japanese (people of the sun) fight us for northern territory; Americans cross the Pacific eastward, stirring up storms. 'Fourteen-one-heart' forms the character 'De' (德, Germany) — the Germans rise with ambition; 'water goes west' alludes to 'Fa' (法, France) — war erupts from the West.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 「太阳」指日本（日本国旗为太阳），「争北土」指日本侵占中国东北（1931年九一八事变）。「美人东渡」指美国参战太平洋战争（1941年）。「十四一心」拆字合为「德」——指纳粹德国。「水去西方」暗指「法」——法国。准确预言了二战主要参战国。
prophecies.verdict_en: 'People of the sun' refers to Japan (the Rising Sun flag); 'contend for northern lands' refers to Japan's occupation of Manchuria (1931 Mukden Incident). 'Americans cross eastward' alludes to U.S. entry into the Pacific War (1941). 'Fourteen-one-heart' deconstructs into the character '德' (De/Germany) — Nazi Germany. 'Water goes west' alludes to '法' (Fa/France). Accurately prophesies the major belligerents of WWII.

prophecies.a:
晋有出头宁坐视，中央生草不堪耘。切齿雠仇今始复，坚固金城壹旦倾。除非携手马先生，马腾四海似苏秦。游说辩才世罕有，掉他三寸舌风生。

白话文：山西方面（阎锡山）岂能坐视不管，中央政权内部杂草丛生难以治理。切齿之仇今日终于报复，看似坚固的金城一旦倾覆。除非携手「马先生」——马歇尔将军奔走四方如同战国苏秦，凭着世间罕有的游说辩才，三寸之舌搅动风云。
prophecies.a_en:
Can Jin (Shanxi) stand idle and watch? Weeds grow in the Central government, too tangled to tend. Teeth-gnashing vengeance is finally repaid today; the seemingly solid golden city collapses overnight. Unless one joins hands with Mr. Ma — Ma gallops across the four seas like Su Qin. His powers of persuasion are rarely seen in this world; his three-inch tongue stirs up a storm.

Vernacular: The Shanxi faction (Yan Xishan) cannot simply stand by; the central government is overgrown with weeds beyond tending. Long-nursed bitter hatred is at last avenged; the seemingly impregnable fortress falls in a single day. Unless one allies with 'Mr. Ma' — General Marshall, who ranges across the world like the ancient diplomat Su Qin, possessing powers of rhetoric the world has seldom seen, his eloquent tongue raising winds wherever it goes.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 「切齿雠仇今始复」指抗战胜利、日本投降（1945年）。「马先生」被解读为美国特使乔治·马歇尔（George Marshall），1945年底至1947年初赴华调停国共冲突，「马腾四海似苏秦」形容其穿梭外交，与史实吻合。
prophecies.verdict_en: 'Teeth-gnashing vengeance finally repaid' refers to victory over Japan and its surrender (1945). 'Mr. Ma' is interpreted as U.S. envoy George Marshall, who mediated between the Nationalists and Communists from late 1945 to early 1947. 'Ma gallops across four seas like Su Qin' describes his shuttle diplomacy, consistent with historical events.

prophecies.a:
得与联军说事因，东人首肯易调停。青天白日由西落，五星旗帜向东生。

白话文：与盟军交涉说明缘由，东方人首先同意便于调停。青天白日旗从西方落下，五星旗帜在东方升起。
prophecies.a_en:
Having explained matters to the allied forces, the eastern people nod in agreement — mediation proceeds with ease. The blue-sky white-sun flag sets in the west; the five-starred banner rises in the east.

Vernacular: After explaining the situation to the allied forces, the eastern people agree and mediation becomes smoother. The Blue Sky with a White Sun flag (Nationalist) descends in the west; the Five-Star Red Flag rises in the east.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 「青天白日由西落」指中华民国国民党政权败退台湾（青天白日旗为国民党旗）。「五星旗帜向东生」指1949年中华人民共和国成立、五星红旗升起。此句是乩文中最著名的预言之一。注意：1933年原报刊版本写「五色旗帜」而非「五星旗帜」，1951年再版时才改为「五星」，存在事后修改的可能。
prophecies.verdict_en: 'Blue-sky white-sun sets in the west' refers to the Nationalist (KMT) government's retreat to Taiwan (the Blue Sky with a White Sun is the KMT flag). 'Five-starred banner rises in the east' refers to the founding of the PRC in 1949 and the raising of the Five-Star Red Flag. This is one of the most famous lines. Note: the original 1933 newspaper version reads 'five-colored flags' (五色旗帜), not 'five-starred'; the change to 'five-starred' appeared only in the 1951 reprint, raising suspicions of post-hoc alteration.

prophecies.a:
二蒋相争一蒋伤，两陈相遇一陈亡。

白话文：两个姓蒋的人相争，其中一个蒋受到伤害；两个姓陈的人相遇，其中一个陈灭亡。
prophecies.q_en: Civil War: The Two Chiangs and Two Chens
prophecies.a_en:
Two Chiangs contend — one Chiang is wounded; two Chens meet — one Chen perishes.

Vernacular: Two people surnamed Chiang struggle against each other, and one is harmed; two people surnamed Chen confront each other, and one is destroyed.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 「二蒋」常被解读为蒋介石与蒋经国父子，或蒋介石与中共方面某「蒋」姓人物。「两陈」有多种解读：陈诚与陈毅、陈立夫与陈伯达等。具体指代存在争议，但整体反映了国共内战中两方阵营的对抗格局。
prophecies.verdict_en: 'Two Chiangs' is often interpreted as Chiang Kai-shek and his son Chiang Ching-kuo, or Chiang Kai-shek versus a CCP figure surnamed Chiang. 'Two Chens' has multiple interpretations: Chen Cheng vs. Chen Yi, Chen Lifu vs. Chen Boda, etc. The specific referents are debated, but the verse broadly reflects the confrontation between the two sides in the Chinese Civil War.

prophecies.a:
东土不如西土乐，五羊风雨见悲伤。水巷仍须是乐邦，诸生不用走忙忙。钱财散尽犹小事，性命安全谢上苍。

白话文：东方大陆不如西方安乐，广州（五羊城）在风雨中充满悲伤。水巷（澳门或香港）仍然是安乐之地，各位不必慌忙奔逃。钱财散尽不过是小事，性命能够保全就该感谢上天了。
prophecies.q_en: Southern Refuge and Hong Kong / Macau
prophecies.a_en:
The eastern land is not as joyful as the western; the City of Five Rams weathers storms of sorrow. The water lanes shall remain a land of contentment — you need not rush about in panic. Wealth squandered is but a small matter; if life is preserved, give thanks to heaven above.

Vernacular: The eastern mainland is not as peaceful as the western regions; Guangzhou (City of Five Rams) endures storms of grief. The water lanes (Macau or Hong Kong) remain a haven of peace — there is no need to flee in haste. Losing one's fortune is a trivial matter; preserving one's life is cause to thank the heavens.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 1949年前后，大量人口从大陆逃往香港、澳门避难。「五羊」即广州，1949年10月广州解放前局势动荡。「水巷」被广泛解读为香港或澳门（英葡殖民地），当时确为大陆难民的避风港。「钱财散尽犹小事，性命安全谢上苍」真实反映了逃难者的心态。
prophecies.verdict_en: Around 1949, large numbers of people fled from the mainland to Hong Kong and Macau. 'Five Rams' is Guangzhou, where the situation was turbulent before its fall in October 1949. 'Water lanes' is widely interpreted as Hong Kong or Macau (British/Portuguese colonies), which were indeed refuges for mainland refugees. 'Wealth lost is a small matter; give thanks for life preserved' authentically captures the refugee mentality.

prophecies.a:
今宵略说言和语，留与明宵话短长。

白话文：今晚暂且说些和平的话语，留待明天再论说长短。
prophecies.a_en:
Tonight we speak briefly of peace; leave it to tomorrow night to discuss what is short and long.

Vernacular: Tonight let us speak briefly of reconciliation; the reckoning of gains and losses shall be left for another night.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 此句为第一部分（1933年刊出）的结尾。「言和语」暗示短暂的和平，「留与明宵话短长」暗示未来还有纷争——与1949年后两岸分治、对峙的局面吻合。同时也是乩文形式上的分段标记，预告还有后续内容。
prophecies.verdict_en: This line closes the first section (published 1933). 'Speaking of peace' suggests a temporary truce; 'leave the reckoning for tomorrow night' implies future conflicts — consistent with the cross-strait standoff after 1949. It also serves as a structural marker in the divination text, signaling that more content follows.

prophecies.a:
天下纷纷各自忙，有馀自足好商量。谁知平地风波起，又见戈兵相对张。

白话文：天下人纷纷各自忙碌，有余粮自给自足还好商量。谁知平地里突然风波骤起，又见到兵戈相向的场面。
prophecies.a_en:
All under heaven bustle about, each with their own affairs; those with surplus and self-sufficiency may negotiate at ease. Who knew that storms would rise on level ground — once again we see weapons raised in confrontation.

Vernacular: Everyone under heaven is busy with their own concerns; those with enough provisions may still discuss terms. But who could have predicted sudden turmoil erupting on flat ground — once more we witness armies arrayed against each other.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 「其二」部分1951年首次刊出，真实性存疑。「平地风波起」「戈兵相对张」常被解读为1966年文化大革命等政治运动中的社会动荡。但此段语句较为笼统，可适用于多个历史时期，缺乏如第一部分般的具体指向性。
prophecies.verdict_en: Part Two was first published in 1951; its authenticity is questionable. 'Storms on level ground' and 'weapons raised in confrontation' are often interpreted as the social upheaval of the Cultural Revolution (1966). However, these lines are rather generic and could apply to multiple historical periods, lacking the specificity of Part One.

prophecies.a:
稻粱难足要金汤，干戈仍是闹忙忙。人逢小辈应恭让，天降贤人有主张。

白话文：粮食难以满足百姓需要金汤（稳固根基），纷争仍然纷纷扰扰。遇到后辈应当恭敬谦让，上天降下贤人自有主张。
prophecies.a_en:
Rice and grain are hard to come by — one needs a solid foundation; strife and conflict still keep everyone busy. When meeting the younger generation, one should be humble and yield; heaven sends down a worthy person with a clear vision.

Vernacular: Food is insufficient and a firm foundation is needed; conflicts and disputes continue to keep everyone occupied. When encountering the younger generation, one should defer respectfully; heaven will send a worthy leader with a definite plan.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 「稻粱难足」可对应1959-1961年大饥荒时期粮食极度匮乏的历史。「天降贤人有主张」常被解读为1978年邓小平推行改革开放。但此部分为1951年刊出版本，对后续数十年事件的「预言」可能受到质疑。
prophecies.verdict_en: 'Rice and grain hard to come by' may correspond to the extreme food shortages during the Great Famine (1959-1961). 'Heaven sends a worthy person with clear vision' is often interpreted as Deng Xiaoping's Reform and Opening Up policy in 1978. However, this section was published in 1951, and its 'prophecies' of events decades later are subject to skepticism.

prophecies.a:
港澳回归母亲抱，一统山河乐太平。

白话文：港澳回归祖国母亲的怀抱，统一的山河享受太平之乐。
prophecies.q_en: Part Two: Hong Kong and Macau Handover
prophecies.a_en:
Hong Kong and Macau return to the mother's embrace; the unified land and rivers enjoy great peace.

Vernacular: Hong Kong and Macau return to the motherland's embrace; the unified mountains and rivers enjoy the joy of peace.
prophecies.verdict_zh: 香港1997年、澳门1999年分别回归中国，与「港澳回归」吻合。但此句被认为极可能是后人增补——1951年版本中并无如此直白的表述，此句主要出现在网络流传的扩展版本中，真实性高度存疑。
prophecies.verdict_en: Hong Kong returned to China in 1997 and Macau in 1999, matching 'Hong Kong and Macau return.' However, this line is widely believed to be a later addition — the 1951 version contained no such explicit statement. This line appears mainly in extended online versions, and its authenticity is highly doubtful.

prophecies.a:
拯患救难是唯一，政教合一乐无忧。诸君各自顾前程，好向灵山勤修炼。

白话文：拯救苦难是唯一的出路，政治与教化合一则天下安乐无忧。诸位各自为前程打算，应当向灵山勤加修炼。
prophecies.a_en:
Rescuing the suffering is the one true way; when governance and faith unite, there is joy without sorrow. Let each of you look to your own future path — go diligently to the Spirit Mountain to cultivate and refine.

Vernacular: Saving those in suffering is the sole path forward; when governance and spiritual teaching become one, the world will know peace and joy. Everyone should attend to their own destiny — go to the Spirit Mountain and practice diligently.
